mnemonics: DMA B.I.G. (Direct Memory Access Bus Is Great) - Helps remember DMA uses the system bus, interrupting the CPU, to grant I/O devices direct access to memory.
keyPoints: Polling is a simple but inefficient method where the CPU constantly checks device status.
Interrupt-driven I/O allows devices to signal the CPU when they need service, freeing up CPU time.
Direct Memory Access (DMA) allows devices to transfer data directly to/from memory without continuous CPU involvement.
commonMistakes: Confusing vectored and non-vectored interrupts; vectored interrupts provide the address of the ISR, while non-vectored do not.
Assuming DMA does not use the system bus; it does, and this can cause CPU contention.
